🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Nenagh Castle
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* the heartlands
*County:* County Tipperary
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th century
*Coords:* 52.8632, -8.1995
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nenagh_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Nenagh%20Castle/@52.8619,-8.1964,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/72/Centre_of_Nenagh_-_panoramio.jpg/330px-Centre_of_Nenagh_-_panoramio.jpg
*Gallery:* 8 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Nenagh is the county town of County Tipperary in Ireland. Nenagh used to be a market town, and the site of the East Munster Ormond Fair.

_Listing 1 of 260_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[🔧 STILL IN PROGRESS\]

*Name:* Layde Church
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* ulster and the north
*County:* County Antrim
*Type:* abbey
*Period:* 13th century
*Coords:* 55.205, -6.035
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Late_church_cantatas_by_Johann_Sebastian_Bach
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Layde%20Church/@55.205,-6.035,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/layde-church.jpg
*Gallery:* 4 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* no
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* no wikipedia page. 

*Description:*
The late church cantatas by Johann Sebastian Bach are sacred cantatas he composed after his fourth cycle of 1728–29. Whether Bach still composed a full cantata cycle in the last 20 years of his life is not known, but the extant cantatas of this period written for occasions of the liturgical year are sometimes referred to as his fifth cycle, as, according to his obituary, he would have written five such cycles – inasmuch as such cantatas were not late additions to earlier cycles, or were adopted in his oratorios.

_Listing 2 of 260_

listing.name: Layde Church
listing.desc: The late church cantatas by Johann Sebastian Bach are sacred cantatas he composed after his fourth cycle of 1728–29. Whether Bach still composed a full cantata cycle in the last 20 years of his life is not known, but the extant cantatas of this period written for occasions of the liturgical year are sometimes referred to as his fifth cycle, as, according to his obituary, he would have written five such cycles – inasmuch as such cantatas were not late additions to earlier cycles, or were adopted in his oratorios.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Ballindoon Priory
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* wild atlantic way
*County:* County Sligo
*Type:* abbey
*Period:* Medieval
*Coords:* 54.082832, -8.323308
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ballindoon_Friary
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Ballindoon%20Priory/@54.099,-8.407,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/ballindoon-priory.jpg
*Gallery:* 4 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Ballindoon Friary was a Dominican priory beside Lough Arrow in County Sligo, Ireland. It was dedicated to St. Mary and founded in 1507 by Thomas O'Farrell. It was dissolved c. 1585 and is now in ruins.

_Listing 3 of 260_

listing.name: Ballindoon Priory

*Description:*
Corby Castle is a Grade I listed building and ancestral home of a cadet branch of the prominent Howard family situated on the southern edge of the village of Great Corby in northern Cumbria, England.

_Listing 4 of 260_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Pengersick Castle
*Country:* england
*Region:* south west
*County:* Cornwall
*Type:* tower house
*Period:* 16th century
*Coords:* 50.1064209, -5.3833807
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pengersick_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Pengersick%20Castle/@50.0917,-5.3583,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/places/ANXAkqEVg9hZefO_bxAk2jsQJ1iTzS6aZISUL4WbyzlBZZIdCWDoT7_gj0tDlAr4dM9lVNQIdiBBLaHIqhXqkfyhQuESs_HVyoLDE28=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://www.pengersickcastle.com/

*Description:*
Pengersick Castle is a fortified manor house located between the villages of Germoe and Praa Sands, in the civil parish of Breage, in Cornwall, England. The tower house, which is in the parish of Breage, is a Grade I listed building. Parts of the building date from the early 16th century.

_Listing 5 of 260_
